_comment: Configurable signal weights for the Sentiment Aggregator. Weights are normalized automatically. Edit these to tune how much each source influences the composite score.

_sources.polymarket: Real-money prediction markets — highest reliability, skin-in-the-game signal
_sources.market_data: Live financial market proxies (FXI, VIX, Copper, AUD/USD) — objective price signals
_sources.news_rss: RSS news feed sentiment (Reuters, SCMP, FT, WSJ) — directional but noisy
_sources.fear_greed: CNN Fear & Greed Index — market psychology gauge
_sources.northbound_flow: ETF volume-based northbound flow proxy — institutional inflow estimate
_sources.social_sentiment: Reddit investor sentiment — retail mood, highest noise
_sources.macro_indicators: World Bank economic indicators — structural/lagging signal

news_heavy._note: Emphasize news sentiment — use during earnings season or policy announcements

market_only._note: Use only objective market price signals — minimal noise from text sources

sentiment_focus._note: Emphasize crowd sentiment — useful for contrarian analysis

_instructions: Copy the weights from your preferred profile into the root level.
Or edit the root-level weights directly.
The analyzer reads root-level keys first, then falls back to 'default' profile.
All weights should sum to 1.0 (the analyzer normalizes anyway, but it's good practice).
Set a source weight to 0 to completely exclude it from analysis.
